```text
What is herdr?
- Herdr is a terminal/agent-focused application with native Windows support in "experimental beta".  
- Quote: "Native Windows support is experimental beta."

How to install on Windows (beta)
- Run PowerShell installer (exact command):
  "powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -c \"irm https://herdr.dev/install.ps1 | iex\""
- The Windows builds ship only through the preview channel and the installer defaults to preview:
  "The Windows installer defaults to preview, writes `channel = \"preview\"` to Herdr’s config"

Package-manager availability / download URLs
- No scoop/winget/cargo/npm package manager installs are mentioned in the document.  
- Direct installer script URL: https://herdr.dev/install.ps1  
- Project/GitHub page: https://github.com/ogulcancelik/herdr

Configuration file locations and format (Windows beta)
- Installer writes channel = "preview" into Herdr’s config (TOML-like key/value shown): channel = "preview"  
- Release storage location: %USERPROFILE%\.herdr\packages\standalone\releases  
- Install junction / bin pointer: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Herdr\bin → current release  
- Example UI setting given for cursor: [ui]host_cursor = "native" (TOML-style)

Prerequisites and notable implementation details / caveats for Windows beta
- Herdr uses ConPTY and Windows process/runtime behavior instead of Unix PTY.  
  Quote: "Herdr on Windows uses ConPTY and Windows process/runtime behavior"  
- Some features map cleanly; others do not — this is a preview for learning real-world issues.  
- Known limitations / unsupported on Windows beta (selected items listed):
  - Direct terminal attach — unsupported  
  - `herdr --remote` from the Windows binary — unsupported  
  - Live server handoff — unsupported  
  - Unix foreground process groups and Unix fd handoff — unsupported  
  - Remote clipboard image bridge — unsupported  
  - Prefix input-source switching and signed binary / SmartScreen avoidance — unsupported  
  (Source lists many unsupported capabilities under "Not supported on Windows beta".)
- Behavior notes / partial support:
  - Live cwd after shell cd: partial; use Herdr integrations or prompt shell integration for live cwd.  
  - Clipboard image paste to agents: unverified.  
  - Kitty graphics: unverified/experimental; keep experimental.kitty_graphics = false unless testing.  
- Cursor and input caveats:
  - Default: Herdr uses a drawn cursor on Windows (host_cursor = "auto") to avoid flicker; you can opt into outer terminal cursor via [ui]host_cursor = "native".  
  - Modified keys vary by terminal; shift+enter may not be distinct unless terminal reports it.

Reporting Windows beta issues (what to include)
- Include: Herdr version, Windows version, terminal app, shell (PowerShell or cmd), whether named HERDR_SESSION was used, relevant Herdr logs, and exact reproduction steps.

Sources / links referenced
- Installer script: https://herdr.dev/install.ps1  
- GitHub: https://github.com/ogulcancelik/herdr

(All answers drawn only from the supplied page content.)

```text
40	
41	Why mise didn't hold up here:
42	
43	- **Shims / activate don't reliably expose global tools.** `mise activate`'s
44	  pwsh prompt-hook and the shims dir both failed to put globally-pinned tools
45	  (`bun`, `go`) on PATH in practice — `mise use -g bun` "succeeded" yet `bun`
46	  wasn't found.
47	- **Arch gaps.** On Windows-on-ARM, `mise use -g bun` records the version but
48	  ships **no windows-arm64 bun binary**, leaving a dead shim.
49	- **It fought the machine's existing node.** A pre-existing `nvm` shadowed
50	  mise's node, so npm used the wrong one.
51	
52	scoop shims live in `~/scoop/shims`, which scoop persists to the user PATH (and
53	`profile.d/00_env.ps1` also prepends) — so `node` (`nodejs-lts`), `bun`, `go`,
54	`rustup`, `ruby` are simply **on PATH**, no activation dance. **Python** is
55	uv-managed (`uv python install --default --preview`), which also drops unversioned
56	`python`/`python3` into `~/.local/bin`.
57	
58	The tradeoff: **no per-project runtime pinning** (mise's headline feature). For a
59	Windows dev box that's a fair trade for "the tools are actually on PATH." If you
60	want per-project versions, `scoop install mise` and use it directly — nothing
61	here depends on it. The full debugging trail lives in
62	`backlog/windows-arm64-managed-machine-rough-edges.md`.
63	
64	## Terminal multiplexer: WezTerm, not tmux/zellij
65	
66	`tmux` and `zellij` are **Unix-only** — there are no native Windows builds — so
67	they're absent here (you can still run them inside WSL). For a native tmux-like
68	experience on Windows the options are:
69	
70	| Option | Splits / tabs | Detach + persist | Notes |
71	|---|---|---|---|
72	| **WezTerm** | yes (panes/tabs, Lua `wezterm.mux`) | yes (mux server + `wezterm connect`) | native Windows; closest to tmux |
73	| Windows Terminal | yes (panes/tabs) | no | great terminal, no session persistence |
74	| Alacritty | no | no | fast, minimal; pair with a multiplexer |
75	| tmux / zellij | — | — | Unix-only; use under WSL |
76	
77	We install **WezTerm** (`wez.wezterm`) as the multiplexer answer: a native
78	Windows terminal with a built-in multiplexer — split panes, tabs, and a
79	persistent mux server you can detach from and reconnect to (`wezterm connect`),
80	plus scriptable layouts via its Lua `wezterm.mux` API. It isn't a 1:1 tmux (the
81	session/persistence model differs), but it's the closest native experience.
82	Windows Terminal stays installed for its panes/tabs; Alacritty stays as the fast
83	minimal option.
84	
85	## starship, not oh-my-posh
86	
87	Both are prompt engines; running both is redundant. We use **starship** because the
88	exact same `starship.toml` already drives the macOS and Linux dotfiles — one config,
89	one behavior everywhere (`starship init powershell`). oh-my-posh is excellent and
90	very PowerShell-native, but adopting it would mean a second, Windows-only prompt
91	config to maintain for no functional gain here.
92	
93	## A parallel PowerShell tree, not a ported shell layer
94	
95	The macOS/Linux dotfiles have a large POSIX `shell/*.sh` layer. Mechanically
96	translating it to PowerShell would be a maintenance sink (two dialects of the same
97	logic drifting apart). Instead the PowerShell config is written **natively** in
98	`profile.d/*.ps1` — idiomatic pwsh, only the *behaviors* shared, not the code.
99	
100	## Native `copilot-proxy`, not a bash shim
101	
102	The `copilot-proxy` tool series is reimplemented as a real PowerShell module
103	(`Invoke-RestMethod`, `Start-Process`, `ConvertFrom-Json`) rather than shelling out
104	to the original bash. Only the Bun throttle shim (pure JS) is reused verbatim. See
105	[copilot-proxy](copilot-proxy.md).
106	
107	## XDG paths, not `%APPDATA%`
108	
109	Windows and XDG split directories on different axes — Windows by whether a
```

38	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
39	function Have($n) { [bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) }
40	function Register-Failure($what) { $script:Failures.Add($what); Write-Warning "FAILED (skipped): $what" }
41	
42	# --- scoop -----------------------------------------------------------------
43	function Test-Admin {
44	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).
45	    try {
46	        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
47	        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
48	    } catch { $false }
49	}
50	
51	function Ensure-Scoop {
52	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
53	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
54	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
55	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
56	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
57	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
58	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
59	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
60	        if (Test-Admin) {
61	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
62	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
63	        } else {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
65	            & $installer
66	        }
67	    }
68	    foreach ($b in 'main', 'extras', 'versions', 'nerd-fonts') {
69	        scoop bucket add $b *> $null
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
74	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
75	}
76	
77	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
78	    if (-not $apps) { return }
79	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
80	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
81	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
82	    # when only "mise" was missing.
83	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
84	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
85	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
86	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
87	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
88	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
89	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
90	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
91	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	# --- winget ----------------------------------------------------------------
96	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
97	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
98	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
99	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
100	    Info "winget install $id"
101	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
102	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
103	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
104	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
105	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
106	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
107	    }
108	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
109	}

111	# Microsoft Store packages (installed by Store product id via the msstore
112	# source) — used for apps with no community winget manifest, e.g. Raycast and the
113	# official ChatGPT desktop app. msstore ignores --scope, so we don't pass it.
114	function Winget-InstallStore([string]$id) {
115	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
116	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
117	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
118	    Info "winget (msstore) install $id"
119	    winget install --id $id -e --source msstore --silent `

122	}

162	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a

164	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
165	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
166	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
167	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
168	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
169	if (Have gh) {
170	    gh auth status *> $null
171	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
172	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
173	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {
174	            Info 'gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash'
175	            gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash
176	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gh-ext:gh-dash' }
177	        }
178	    } else {
179	        Info 'gh not authenticated -- skipping gh-dash (gh auth login, then reapply)'
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	# node/bun come from scoop above (on ~/scoop/shims, reliably on PATH). We do NOT
184	# use mise here -- see the runtimes note in the core scoop list.
185	
186	# Default Python, uv-managed. --default also creates unversioned python/python3
187	# (not just python3.x) in UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR (~/.local/bin, on PATH via 00_env
188	# ahead of the Store's python.exe app-alias). On uv 0.8+ `--default` lives behind
189	# preview mode, so it must be paired with `--preview` (older uv just ignored it) —
190	# without it, current uv errors: "`--default` is only available in preview mode".
191	if (Have uv) {
192	    Info 'uv python install --default --preview'
193	    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
194	    uv python install --default --preview 2>&1 | Out-Host
195	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
196	}
197	
198	# Make PSGallery usable non-interactively before Install-Module (NuGet provider
199	# + trust) so the fzf key bindings / audio cmdlets install reliably.
200	try {
201	    if (-not (Get-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -ListAvailable -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
202	        Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
203	    }
204	    if ((Get-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).InstallationPolicy -ne 'Trusted') {
205	        Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
206	    }
207	} catch { Write-Warning "PSGallery prep failed (non-fatal): $_" }
208	
209	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d
210	# (Ctrl+t = files, Ctrl+r = fuzzy history search).
211	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
212	    Info 'Install-Module PSFzf'
213	    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
214	}
215	# AudioDeviceCmdlets: absolute volume + explicit mute for the sys* helpers.
216	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name AudioDeviceCmdlets)) {
217	    Info 'Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets'
218	    Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
219	}
220	
221	{{ if .installExtraRuntimes -}}
222	# Extra language runtimes, native via scoop (rust via rustup). node/bun/uv are
223	# baseline; go/rust/ruby added here.
224	Scoop-Install @('go', 'rustup', 'ruby')
225	{{ end -}}
226	
227	{{ if .installMediaTools -}}
228	Scoop-Install @('ffmpeg', 'imagemagick')
229	{{ end -}}
230	
231	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
232	# AI coding agents (npm-based CLIs; Antigravity IDE goes via winget below).
233	Npm-InstallGlobal @(
234	    '@anthropic-ai/claude-code',
235	    'opencode-ai',
236	    '@openai/codex',
237	    '@github/copilot'
238	)
239	# NOTE: SpecStory ships no native-Windows CLI (macOS: brew; Linux/WSL: GitHub
240	# release binaries only), so it is intentionally omitted -- `npm i -g
241	# @specstory/cli` 404s. The Copilot module already no-ops when `specstory` is
242	# absent. Experimental Windows build from the unmerged PR #191:
243	# `just specstory-build` (see backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md).
244	
245	# OpenCode Desktop (GUI companion to the opencode-ai CLI) via scoop extras.
246	Scoop-Install @('extras/opencode-desktop')
247	{{ end -}}
248	
249	{{ if .installWindowsApps -}}
250	# GUI apps + editors via winget.
251	foreach ($id in @(
252	    'Microsoft.VisualStudioCode',
253	    'Anysphere.Cursor',
254	    'Microsoft.WindowsTerminal',
255	    'Alacritty.Alacritty',
256	    'wez.wezterm',
257	    'Microsoft.PowerToys',
258	    'Google.Antigravity',
259	    'Docker.DockerDesktop',
260	    'Discord.Discord',
261	    # AI desktop app + browsers.
262	    'Anthropic.Claude',
263	    'Google.Chrome',
264	    'TheBrowserCompany.Arc',
265	    'Zen-Team.Zen-Browser'
266	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
267	    'Grammarly.Grammarly'   # skipped on managed machines (often policy-blocked)
268	{{- end }}
269	)) { Winget-Install $id }
270	
271	# Store-only apps: Raycast (default launcher) + the official ChatGPT desktop app.
272	# Neither has a community winget manifest, so they install by Store product id.
273	# Codex is the npm @openai/codex CLI (coding-agents block above), not a Store app.
274	# NB: 9PLM9XGG6VKS is the OpenAI "ChatGPT" listing; the old 9NT1R1C2HH7J was
275	# "ChatGPT Classic", which winget's msstore source no longer resolves ("no
276	# matching package"). Confirm live ids with `winget search --source msstore ...`.
277	Winget-InstallStore '9PFXXSHC64H3'   # Raycast
278	Winget-InstallStore '9PLM9XGG6VKS'   # ChatGPT (OpenAI)
279	
280	# Nerd Fonts so starship glyphs render. Hack-NF-Mono supplies the
281	# "Hack Nerd Font Mono" family the Alacritty / Windows Terminal / editor
282	# configs reference; Hack-NF ("Hack Nerd Font") is the proportional companion.
283	Scoop-Install @('Hack-NF', 'Hack-NF-Mono')
284	
285	Write-Warning 'Raycast is the default launcher but shares Alt+Space with PowerToys Run -- disable PowerToys Run (PowerToys Settings > PowerToys Run > off) or rebind a hotkey. Raycast for Windows is a Store beta; if it fails to install, Flow Launcher is the fallback: winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher.'
286	{{ end -}}
287	
288	{{ if .installGamingApps -}}
289	Winget-Install 'Valve.Steam'
290	{{ end -}}
291	
292	{{ if .installUtilityApps -}}
293	# System / utility apps via winget.
294	foreach ($id in @(
295	    'CPUID.CPU-Z',
296	    'TechPowerUp.GPU-Z',
297	    'REALiX.HWiNFO',
298	    'JAMSoftware.TreeSize.Free',
299	    'WinDirStat.WinDirStat',
300	    'voidtools.Everything',
301	    'ShareX.ShareX',
302	    'VideoLAN.VLC',
303	    'OBSProject.OBSStudio',
304	    'Rufus.Rufus'
305	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
306	    'Tailscale.Tailscale'   # skipped on managed machines (org policy blocks the MSI, exit 1625)
307	{{- end }}
308	)) { Winget-Install $id }
309	{{ end -}}
310	
311	{{ if .installLlmTools -}}
312	# Local LLM tools: Ollama (winget) + LiteLLM (uv tool).
313	Winget-Install 'Ollama.Ollama'
314	if (Have uv) { Info 'uv tool install litellm'; uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:litellm' } }
315	{{ end -}}
316	
317	{{ if .installTunnelTools -}}
318	Scoop-Install @('ngrok', 'cloudflared')
319	{{ end -}}
320	
321	{{ if .installIacTools -}}
322	Scoop-Install @('terraform', 'opentofu')
323	Winget-Install 'Microsoft.AzureCLI'
324	# Azure DevOps CLI (az extension -> `az devops`, `az repos`, `az pipelines`).
325	# It also auto-installs on first `az devops`/`az repos` use; pre-adding here just
326	# skips the first-use prompt. Skipped when az isn't on PATH yet (a fresh winget
327	# install needs a new shell) -- harmless, since first use self-installs it.
328	if (Have az) {
329	    Info 'az extension add azure-devops'
330	    az extension add --name azure-devops --upgrade --only-show-errors
331	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'az-ext:azure-devops' }
332	}
333	{{ end -}}
334	
335	if ($script:Failures.Count -gt 0) {
336	    Write-Warning ("package install: {0} item(s) failed and were skipped:" -f $script:Failures.Count)
337	    $script:Failures | ForEach-Object { Write-Warning "  - $_" }
338	    Write-Warning "Re-run 'just apply' (or 'chezmoi apply') to retry, or install them manually."
339	} else {
340	    Info 'package install complete — no failures'
341	}
342

1	# Windows dotfiles — chezmoi config template.
2	# Rendered once at `chezmoi init`; answers are stored in
3	# %USERPROFILE%\.config\chezmoi\chezmoi.toml and never re-asked.
4	#
5	# This repo targets **native Windows + PowerShell 7 (pwsh)** only. There is no
6	# OS branching: everything here assumes Windows. WSL/macOS/Linux are handled by
7	# the separate cross-platform dotfiles repo.
8	
9	[data]
10	    # role picks coarse defaults; every toggle can still be overridden.
11	    {{- $role := promptChoiceOnce . "role" "Role: workstation (full desktop) or minimal (shell only)" (list "workstation" "minimal") "workstation" }}
12	    {{- $full := eq $role "workstation" }}
13	    role  = {{ $role | quote }}
14	    name  = {{ promptStringOnce . "name" "Your full name (git)" "Da-Wei Lee" | quote }}
15	    email = {{ promptStringOnce . "email" "Your git email" "daviddwlee84@gmail.com" | quote }}
16	
17	    # --- feature toggles (role-defaulted) ---
18	    installCodingAgents  = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installCodingAgents" "Install coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory)" $full }}
19	    installWindowsApps   = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installWindowsApps" "Install GUI apps via winget (VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast)" $full }}
20	    installUtilityApps   = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installUtilityApps" "Install utility apps via winget (CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO)" $full }}
21	    installGamingApps    = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installGamingApps" "Install gaming apps (Steam)" false }}
22	    installExtraRuntimes = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installExtraRuntimes" "Install extra runtimes (rust, go, ruby) beyond node/bun/uv" $full }}
23	    installMediaTools    = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installMediaTools" "Install media CLIs (ffmpeg, imagemagick)" false }}
24	    installLlmTools      = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installLlmTools" "Install local LLM tools (Ollama, LiteLLM)" false }}
25	    installTunnelTools   = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installTunnelTools" "Install tunnel tools (ngrok, cloudflared)" false }}
26	    installIacTools      = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installIacTools" "Install Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu)" false }}
27	    installSshServer     = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installSshServer" "Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)" false }}
28	    useChineseMirror     = {{ promptBoolOnce . "useChineseMirror" "Use China (GFW) mirrors for pip/npm/cargo/go/node" false }}
29	    managedMachine       = {{ promptBoolOnce . "managedMachine" "Managed or corporate machine (skip org-policy-blocked apps like Tailscale and Grammarly)" false }}
30	    backupMode           = {{ promptChoiceOnce . "backupMode" "Back up existing files before apply (smart|full|off)" (list "smart" "full" "off") "smart" | quote }}
31	    enableVimMode        = {{ promptBoolOnce . "enableVimMode" "Enable PSReadLine vi editing mode" true }}
32	
33	# Run .ps1 scripts (.chezmoiscripts + modify_/create_) under PowerShell 7.
34	[interpreters.ps1]
35	    command = "pwsh"
36	    args = ["-NoProfile", "-NonInteractive"]
37	
38	[diff]
39	    exclude = ["scripts"]
40	
41	[status]
42	    exclude = ["scripts"]
43

12	    steps:

14	
15	      - name: Install chezmoi + PowerShell modules
16	        shell: pwsh
17	        run: |
18	          $ok = $false
19	          foreach ($try in 1..3) {
20	            winget install --id twpayne.chezmoi -e --silent --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements
21	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { $ok = $true; break }
22	            Write-Host "winget chezmoi attempt $try failed (exit $LASTEXITCODE); retrying..."
23	            Start-Sleep 8
24	          }
25	          echo "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\WinGet\Links" >> $env:GITHUB_PATH
26	          if (-not $ok) {
27	            Write-Host 'winget failed after retries; falling back to scoop'
28	            irm get.scoop.sh -OutFile "$env:TEMP\scoop.ps1"
29	            & "$env:TEMP\scoop.ps1" -RunAsAdmin
30	            scoop install chezmoi
31	            echo "$HOME\scoop\shims" >> $env:GITHUB_PATH
32	          }
33	          Install-Module PSScriptAnalyzer -Force -Scope CurrentUser
34	          Install-Module Pester -Force -Scope CurrentUser -SkipPublisherCheck
35	          $global:LASTEXITCODE = 0
36	
37	      - name: Lint PowerShell (PSScriptAnalyzer)
38	        shell: pwsh
39	        run: |
40	          $issues = Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Path . -Recurse -Settings ./PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1
41	          $issues | Format-Table -AutoSize
42	          if ($issues | Where-Object Severity -eq 'Error') { throw "PSScriptAnalyzer found $(($issues | Where-Object Severity -eq 'Error').Count) error(s)" }
43	
44	      - name: chezmoi init (writes config from prompt answers)
45	        shell: pwsh
46	        run: |
47	          $flags = @(
48	            '--promptChoice','Role: workstation (full desktop) or minimal (shell only)=minimal',
49	            '--promptString','Your full name (git)=CI Runner',
50	            '--promptString','Your git email=ci@example.com',
51	            '--promptBool','Install coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory)=false',
52	            '--promptBool','Install GUI apps via winget (VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast)=false',
53	            '--promptBool','Install utility apps via winget (CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO)=false',
54	            '--promptBool','Install gaming apps (Steam)=false',
55	            '--promptBool','Install extra runtimes (rust, go, ruby) beyond node/bun/uv=false',
56	            '--promptBool','Install media CLIs (ffmpeg, imagemagick)=false',
57	            '--promptBool','Install local LLM tools (Ollama, LiteLLM)=false',
58	            '--promptBool','Install tunnel tools (ngrok, cloudflared)=false',
59	            '--promptBool','Install Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu)=false',
60	            '--promptBool','Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)=false',
61	            '--promptBool','Use China (GFW) mirrors for pip/npm/cargo/go/node=false',
62	            '--promptBool','Managed or corporate machine (skip org-policy-blocked apps like Tailscale and Grammarly)=false',
63	            '--promptChoice','Back up existing files before apply (smart|full|off)=off',
64	            '--promptBool','Enable PSReadLine vi editing mode=true'
65	          )
66	          chezmoi init --source $PWD --no-tty @flags
67	
68	      - name: Parse-check rendered .ps1.tmpl scripts
69	        shell: pwsh
70	        run: |
71	          $failed = $false
72	          Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.ps1.tmpl | ForEach-Object {
73	            $rendered = Get-Content -Raw $_.FullName | chezmoi execute-template --source .
74	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0 -or -not $rendered) { Write-Host "RENDER FAIL: $($_.Name)"; $failed = $true; return }
75	            $errors = $null
76	            [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseInput($rendered, [ref]$null, [ref]$errors) | Out-Null
77	            if ($errors) { Write-Host "PARSE FAIL: $($_.Name)"; $errors | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  $_" }; $failed = $true }
78	            else { Write-Host "OK:   $($_.Name)" }
79	          }
80	          if ($failed) { throw 'A rendered template failed to render or parse' }
81	
82	      - name: chezmoi apply (files only, no package scripts)
83	        shell: pwsh
84	        run: |
85	          $dest = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP 'dothome'
86	          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest | Out-Null
87	          chezmoi apply --source $PWD --destination $dest --exclude scripts --verbose
88	          Write-Host '--- deployed tree ---'
89	          Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File $dest | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Replace($dest,'') }
90	
91	      - name: Pester tests
92	        shell: pwsh
93	        run: |
94	          if (Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.Tests.ps1) {
95	            Invoke-Pester -CI -Path . -Output Detailed
96	          } else { Write-Host 'no Pester tests yet' }
97

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`

```text
1	# 10_tools.ps1 — activate CLI tools. Each block no-ops if the tool is absent,
2	# so a partial install (or the minimal role) never errors the prompt.
3	#
4	# Tool `init` output (starship/zoxide/atuin/direnv/tv) is cached under
5	# $XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init so a warm shell dot-sources a static file instead of
6	# cold-spawning the binary on every start — the dominant pwsh-startup cost on a
7	# fresh box (each spawn is a process launch + Defender scan). The cache
8	# auto-regenerates when the tool's exe timestamp changes (e.g. a scoop upgrade);
9	# delete ~/.cache/pwsh-init to force a rebuild.
10	function Import-CachedInit {
11	    param(
12	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name,
13	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Exe,
14	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][scriptblock]$Generate
15	    )
16	    $cmd = Get-Command $Exe -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
17	    if (-not $cmd) { return }
18	    $cacheDir  = Join-Path $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME 'pwsh-init'
19	    $cacheFile = Join-Path $cacheDir "$Name.ps1"
20	    $stampFile = "$cacheFile.stamp"
21	    try { $stamp = (Get-Item -LiteralPath $cmd.Source).LastWriteTimeUtc.Ticks.ToString() } catch { $stamp = '' }
22	    $fresh = (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheFile) -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $stampFile) -and
23	             ((Get-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim() -eq $stamp)
24	    if (-not $fresh) {
25	        try {
26	            $out = & $Generate | Out-String
27	            if (-not $out.Trim()) { return }   # tool errored / empty output — retry next start
28	            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $cacheDir | Out-Null }
29	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Value $out   -Encoding utf8
30	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Value $stamp -Encoding utf8
31	        } catch { return }
32	    }
33	    try { . $cacheFile } catch { Write-Warning "profile: cached init '$Name' failed: $_" }
34	}
35	
36	# starship prompt
37	Import-CachedInit -Name 'starship' -Exe 'starship' -Generate { starship init powershell }
38	
39	# zoxide — smarter cd (rebinds `cd` to `z`)
40	Import-CachedInit -Name 'zoxide' -Exe 'zoxide' -Generate { zoxide init powershell --cmd cd }
41	
42	# Runtimes (node/bun/go/rust/ruby) are native via scoop and Python via uv — no
43	# mise on Windows (its activate/shims are unreliable here). See docs/rationale.
44	
45	# atuin — SQLite shell history with fuzzy search (Ctrl+R)
46	Import-CachedInit -Name 'atuin' -Exe 'atuin' -Generate { atuin init powershell }
47	
48	# fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
49	if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
50	    $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
51	    if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
52	        $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
53	    }
54	    # Import directly — a `Get-Module -ListAvailable` guard scans all of
55	    # PSModulePath (slow, esp. under OneDrive-hydrated Documents\PowerShell\Modules).
56	    # `Get-Module -Name` checks only loaded modules, so it's cheap.
57	    Import-Module PSFzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
58	    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
59	        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	# direnv — per-directory environments
64	Import-CachedInit -Name 'direnv' -Exe 'direnv' -Generate { direnv hook pwsh }
65	
66	# television (tv) — fuzzy picker shell integration. tv 0.15+ renamed the shell
67	# value `powershell` -> `power-shell`; older builds used `powershell`. Try the
68	# new spelling first, fall back, and only cache non-empty output so a version
69	# mismatch never errors the prompt.
70	Import-CachedInit -Name 'tv' -Exe 'tv' -Generate {
71	    $o = tv init power-shell 2>$null | Out-String
72	    if (-not $o.Trim()) { $o = tv init powershell 2>$null | Out-String }
73	    $o
74	}
75
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/35_yazi.ps1`

```text
1	# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
2	# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
3	if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
4	    function y {
5	        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
6	        try {
7	            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
8	            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
9	            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
10	                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
11	            }
12	        } finally {
13	            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	        }
15	    }
16	}
17
```

```text
1	# 30_apps.ps1 — app control, system audio, and clipboard helpers. Native
2	# PowerShell equivalents of the macOS/Linux app*/sys*/x helpers, same verb names.
3	
4	# --- app control (applaunch / appquit / apprestart / apprunning / applist) ---
5	function applaunch {
6	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
7	    Start-Process $Name
8	}
9	function appquit {
10	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
11	    $proc = $Name -replace '\.exe$', ''
12	    Get-Process -Name $proc -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force
13	}
14	function apprestart {
15	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
16	    appquit $Name; Start-Sleep 1; applaunch $Name
17	}
18	function apprunning {
19	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
20	    [bool](Get-Process -Name ($Name -replace '\.exe$', '') -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
21	}
22	function applist {
23	    Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.MainWindowTitle } | Select-Object Name, Id, MainWindowTitle | Sort-Object Name
24	}
25	
26	# --- system audio (sysvol / sysmute) ---
27	# Absolute level + explicit mute use AudioDeviceCmdlets when installed; a
28	# dependency-free media-key fallback covers up/down and mute-toggle everywhere.
29	function script:Initialize-VolumeKeys {
30	    if (-not ('Native.WinVol' -as [type])) {
31	        Add-Type -Name WinVol -Namespace Native -MemberDefinition @'
32	[System.Runtime.InteropServices.DllImport("user32.dll")]
33	public static extern void keybd_event(byte bVk, byte bScan, uint dwFlags, System.UIntPtr dwExtraInfo);
34	'@
35	    }
36	}
37	function script:Send-VolumeKey {
38	    param([byte]$Vk, [int]$Times = 1)
39	    Initialize-VolumeKeys
40	    for ($i = 0; $i -lt $Times; $i++) { [Native.WinVol]::keybd_event($Vk, 0, 0, [System.UIntPtr]::Zero) }
41	}
42	
43	function sysvol {
44	    param([Parameter(Position = 0)][string]$Arg, [Parameter(Position = 1)][int]$Amount = 10)
45	    $hasCmdlet = [bool](Get-Command Set-AudioDevice -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
46	    switch ($Arg) {
47	        'up'   { if ($hasCmdlet) { $c = [int]((Get-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume) -replace '\D'); Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume ([math]::Min(100, $c + $Amount)) } else { Send-VolumeKey 0xAF ([math]::Ceiling($Amount / 2)) } }
48	        'down' { if ($hasCmdlet) { $c = [int]((Get-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume) -replace '\D'); Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume ([math]::Max(0, $c - $Amount)) } else { Send-VolumeKey 0xAE ([math]::Ceiling($Amount / 2)) } }
49	        ''     { if ($hasCmdlet) { Get-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume } else { Write-Warning 'sysvol: install AudioDeviceCmdlets for level readout; use `sysvol up|down`' } }
50	        default {
51	            if ($Arg -match '^\d+$') {
52	                if ($hasCmdlet) { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume ([int]$Arg) }
53	                else { Write-Warning 'sysvol <N> (absolute) needs AudioDeviceCmdlets; use `sysvol up|down` instead' }
54	            } else { Write-Warning "sysvol: usage: sysvol [up|down [step] | <0-100>]" }
55	        }
56	    }
57	}
58	
59	function sysmute {
60	    param([Parameter(Position = 0)][ValidateSet('', 'on', 'off', 'toggle')][string]$State = 'toggle')
61	    if (Get-Command Set-AudioDevice -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
62	        switch ($State) {
63	            'on'  { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackMute $true }
64	            'off' { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackMute $false }
65	            default { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackMuteToggle }
66	        }
67	    } else {
68	        Send-VolumeKey 0xAD 1   # VK_VOLUME_MUTE toggles
69	        if ($State -in 'on', 'off') { Write-Warning 'sysmute on/off needs AudioDeviceCmdlets; toggled instead' }
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	# --- clipboard / open (x) ---
74	# x copy [text]   copy args (or piped stdin) to the clipboard
75	# x paste         print the clipboard
76	# x open <target> open a file/URL with its default handler
77	function x {
78	    param(
79	        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)][ValidateSet('copy', 'paste', 'open')][string]$Action,
80	        [Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Rest
81	    )
82	    switch ($Action) {
83	        'copy'  {
84	            $data = if ($Rest) { $Rest -join ' ' } else { ($input | Out-String).TrimEnd("`r", "`n") }
85	            Set-Clipboard -Value $data
86	        }
87	        'paste' { Get-Clipboard }
88	        'open'  { if ($Rest) { Start-Process ($Rest -join ' ') } else { Start-Process . } }
89	    }
90	}
91
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Tools
2	
3	The install set lives in `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
4	(the single source of truth), gated by the init toggles. **scoop** handles CLI
5	tools; **winget** handles GUI apps.
6	
7	## CLI tools (scoop)
8	
9	| Tool | Purpose |
10	|---|---|
11	| git, gh, glab | version control + GitHub / GitLab CLIs |
12	| neovim | editor (LazyVim, pre-configured) |
13	| lazygit | git TUI (delta pager configured) |
14	| zoxide | smarter `cd` |
15	| fzf, fd, ripgrep | fuzzy find / file search / grep |
16	| bat, eza | `cat` / `ls` replacements |
17	| delta | git diff pager |
18	| jq | JSON processor |
19	| yazi, btop | file manager / system monitor |
20	| television (`tv`) | fuzzy picker / channel launcher |
21	| tldr (tlrc) | community man-page cheatsheets; `tldrf` adds `zh_TW → zh → en` fallback |
22	| gh dash | GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (gh extension; installs once `gh` is authenticated) |
23	| starship | prompt |
24	| node | JS runtime (`nodejs-lts`) |
25	| uv | Python package/runtime manager |
26	| bun | JS runtime + package manager (backs copilot-proxy) |
27	| just | task runner (this repo's `justfile`) |
28	| make | GNU make |
29	| zig | C compiler for Neovim tree-sitter parsers |
30	| gcc | amd64 C compiler (**ARM64 hosts only** — matches the x64-emulated nvim) |
31	| tree-sitter | tree-sitter CLI (nvim-treesitter `main` branch) |
32	| win32yank | clipboard provider for Neovim |
33	| gnupg | `gpg` (git commit signing, verification) |
34	
35	Runtimes are **native (scoop), not mise** — see [rationale](rationale.md#runtimes-scoop-natives-not-mise-on-windows).
36	`node` (`nodejs-lts`) and `bun` come from scoop; `go`, `rust` (rustup), `ruby`
37	are added when **Extra runtimes** is enabled. A default **Python** is uv-managed:
38	`uv python install --default --preview` puts `python`/`python3` in `~/.local/bin`
39	(on PATH ahead of the Store's `python.exe` app-alias), so `python`, `uv run`, and
40	`uv venv` all work without the Microsoft Store.
41	
42	## GUI apps (winget)
43	
44	| App | winget id |
45	|---|---|
46	| VSCode | `Microsoft.VisualStudioCode` |
47	| Cursor | `Anysphere.Cursor` |
48	| Windows Terminal | `Microsoft.WindowsTerminal` |
49	| Alacritty | `Alacritty.Alacritty` |
50	| WezTerm | `wez.wezterm` (tmux-like multiplexer; managed `~/.config/wezterm/wezterm.lua` sets pwsh as the shell + Nerd Font) |
51	| PowerToys | `Microsoft.PowerToys` |
52	| Raycast | `9PFXXSHC64H3` (msstore) |
53	| Antigravity | `Google.Antigravity` |
54	| Docker Desktop | `Docker.DockerDesktop` |
55	| Discord | `Discord.Discord` |
56	| Claude Desktop | `Anthropic.Claude` |
57	| ChatGPT | `9PLM9XGG6VKS` (msstore) |
58	| Chrome | `Google.Chrome` |
59	| Arc | `TheBrowserCompany.Arc` |
60	| Zen Browser | `Zen-Team.Zen-Browser` |
61	| Grammarly | `Grammarly.Grammarly` |
62	| Steam (gaming) | `Valve.Steam` |
63	
64	## Utility apps (winget)
65	
66	Enabled by **Install utility apps**:
67	
68	| App | winget id | What |
69	|---|---|---|
70	| CPU-Z | `CPUID.CPU-Z` | CPU/mainboard info |
71	| GPU-Z | `TechPowerUp.GPU-Z` | GPU info + sensors |
72	| HWiNFO | `REALiX.HWiNFO` | full hardware monitoring |
73	| TreeSize Free | `JAMSoftware.TreeSize.Free` | disk-usage explorer |
74	| WinDirStat | `WinDirStat.WinDirStat` | disk-usage treemap |
75	| Everything | `voidtools.Everything` | instant filename search |
76	| ShareX | `ShareX.ShareX` | screenshots + screen recording |
77	| OBS Studio | `OBSProject.OBSStudio` | screen recording / streaming |
78	| VLC | `VideoLAN.VLC` | media player |
79	| Rufus | `Rufus.Rufus` | bootable USB creator |
80	| Tailscale | `Tailscale.Tailscale` | mesh VPN |
81	
82	!!! note "Launchers & the Alt+Space clash"
83	    **Raycast** (Microsoft Store, id `9PFXXSHC64H3`) is the default launcher.
84	    It shares **Alt+Space** with **PowerToys Run** — disable PowerToys Run
85	    (PowerToys Settings › PowerToys Run › off) or rebind one. Raycast for Windows
86	    is still a Store beta; if it won't install, fall back to
87	    [Flow Launcher](https://www.flowlauncher.com/):
88	    `winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher`.
89	
90	!!! note "Notepad++ is opt-in"
91	    Dropped from the default set: on Windows-on-ARM its installer is a
92	    low-reputation binary that Windows Defender / SmartScreen flags as PUA
93	    (a false positive, aggravated by managed-machine PUA protection), and it's
94	    redundant with VSCode / Cursor / nvim. Add it yourself if you want it:
95	    `winget install Notepad++.Notepad++` or `scoop install notepadplusplus`.
96	
97	!!! note "Managed / corporate machines"
98	    The **Managed machine** init toggle skips apps that org policy commonly
99	    blocks — **Grammarly** and **Tailscale** (its MSI fails with exit `1625`
100	    under policy). Leave the toggle off on a personal machine to install them.
101	
102	!!! note "Alacritty & Nerd Fonts (machine-wide install)"
103	    scoop's `nerd-fonts` bucket registers **Hack Nerd Font Mono** *per-user*
104	    (HKCU). Windows Terminal and WezTerm see per-user fonts, but **Alacritty on
105	    Windows reads only the machine-wide font collection**, so it can't find the
106	    font and falls back to a default. Fix (needs admin):
107	    `just install-fonts-machine-wide` (runs `scoop install -g Hack-NF-Mono Hack-NF`),
108	    then restart Alacritty. Diagnose the family name with
109	    `[System.Drawing.FontFamily]::Families.Name | Select-String Hack`.
110	
111	## AI agents (npm)
112	
113	`@anthropic-ai/claude-code`, `opencode-ai`, `@openai/codex`, `@github/copilot`
114	— installed globally via npm (provided by the scoop `node`). **OpenCode Desktop**
115	(GUI companion to the `opencode-ai` CLI) installs via scoop (`extras/opencode-desktop`).
116	The official **ChatGPT** desktop app installs via the Microsoft Store (see GUI
117	apps); **Codex** is the `@openai/codex` npm CLI above, not a Store app. SpecStory
118	has no native-Windows CLI package, so it's omitted here.
119	
120	## PowerShell modules (PSGallery)
121	
122	- **PSFzf** — fzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history).
123	- **AudioDeviceCmdlets** — absolute volume + mute for the `sysvol`/`sysmute` helpers.
124	
125	## Opt-in dev stacks
126	
127	Off by default; enable the matching init prompt:
128	
129	| Toggle | Installs |
130	|---|---|
131	| Local LLM tools | Ollama (`Ollama.Ollama`) + LiteLLM (`uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'`) |
132	| Tunnel tools | ngrok, cloudflared (scoop) |
133	| IaC tools | Terraform, OpenTofu (scoop) + Azure CLI & the `azure-devops` extension (`az devops`/`az repos`, winget) |
134	| OpenSSH server | Microsoft OpenSSH Server (sshd): Windows capability + auto-start service + inbound TCP 22 firewall rule, with pwsh as the default shell. Needs admin — set up on an elevated `chezmoi apply`, or run `just enable-sshd` from an elevated pwsh. |
135	
136	**China mirrors** routes pip / npm / cargo / go / node package fetches through GFW
137	mirrors (Tsinghua / npmmirror / goproxy.cn / rsproxy), applied both at install time
138	and in the interactive shell (`profile.d/05_mirrors.ps1`).
139	
140	## Television (tv) channels
141	
142	Custom pickers under `%APPDATA%\television\cable\` (run `tv` to browse, or `tv <name>`):
143	
144	| Channel | What |
145	|---|---|
146	| `aliases` | PowerShell aliases & functions (cached at shell startup) |
147	| `git-ops` | common git commands → Enter copies to clipboard |
148	| `ports` | listening TCP ports → Ctrl+K kills the owning process |
149	| `kill-process` | processes by memory → Enter/Ctrl+K kill, Ctrl+D force-kill |
150	| `scoop-apps` | installed scoop apps → Enter info, Ctrl+U update, Ctrl+X uninstall |
151	| `apps` | launch an app from Start Menu shortcuts |
152	| `channels` | browse every tv channel by description → Enter opens the selected one |
153	
154	## Upgrades
155	
156	Install and upgrade are separate. `chezmoi apply` only installs what's missing;
157	upgrades are explicit:
158	
159	```powershell
160	just upgrade-scoop     # scoop update *
161	just upgrade-winget    # winget upgrade --all
162	```
163
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# 工具索引
2

4	（單一事實來源），並由初始化開關控制。**scoop** 負責 CLI 工具；**winget** 負責
5	GUI 應用程式。
6	
7	## CLI 工具（scoop）
8	
9	| 工具 | 用途 |
10	|---|---|
11	| git、gh、glab | 版本控制 + GitHub / GitLab CLI |
12	| neovim | 編輯器（已配置 LazyVim） |
13	| lazygit | git TUI（已配置 delta 分頁器） |
14	| zoxide | 更聰明的 `cd` |
15	| fzf、fd、ripgrep | 模糊搜尋 / 找檔案 / grep |

108	## AI agents（npm）
109	
110	`@anthropic-ai/claude-code`、`opencode-ai`、`@openai/codex`、`@github/copilot`
111	—— 透過 npm 全域安裝（由 scoop 的 `node` 提供）。**OpenCode Desktop**（`opencode-ai`
112	CLI 的 GUI 版）由 scoop 安裝（`extras/opencode-desktop`）。官方 **ChatGPT** 桌面程式
113	透過 Microsoft Store 安裝（見 GUI 應用程式）；**Codex** 是上面的 `@openai/codex` npm
114	CLI，不是 Store app。SpecStory 沒有原生 Windows CLI 套件，故此處略過。

117

121	## 選用開發套件
122	
123	預設關閉；開啟對應的 init 提問即安裝：
124	
125	| 開關 | 安裝內容 |
126	|---|---|
127	| Local LLM tools | Ollama（`Ollama.Ollama`）+ LiteLLM（`uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'`） |
128	| Tunnel tools | ngrok、cloudflared（scoop） |
129	| IaC tools | Terraform、OpenTofu（scoop）+ Azure CLI 與 `azure-devops` 擴充（`az devops`/`az repos`,winget） |
130	| OpenSSH server | Microsoft OpenSSH Server（sshd）：Windows capability + 自動啟動服務 + inbound TCP 22 防火牆規則，並以 pwsh 為預設 shell。需系統管理員 —— 用提升權限的 `chezmoi apply`，或在提升權限的 pwsh 執行 `just enable-sshd`。 |
131	
132	**China mirrors** 會把 pip / npm / cargo / go / node 的套件抓取導向 GFW 鏡像
133	（清華 / npmmirror / goproxy.cn / rsproxy），安裝時與互動式 shell
134	（`profile.d/05_mirrors.ps1`）皆生效。
135	
136	## Television（tv）channels
137	
138	自訂選擇器放在 `%APPDATA%\television\cable\`（執行 `tv` 瀏覽，或 `tv <name>`）：
139	
140	| Channel | 用途 |
141	|---|---|
142	| `aliases` | PowerShell aliases 與函式（shell 啟動時快取） |
143	| `git-ops` | 常用 git 指令 → Enter 複製到剪貼簿 |
144	| `ports` | 監聽中的 TCP ports → Ctrl+K 結束佔用的行程 |
145	| `kill-process` | 依記憶體排序的行程 → Enter/Ctrl+K 結束、Ctrl+D 強制結束 |
146	| `scoop-apps` | 已安裝的 scoop apps → Enter 資訊、Ctrl+U 更新、Ctrl+X 移除 |
147	| `apps` | 從開始功能表捷徑啟動應用程式 |
148	| `channels` | 依描述瀏覽所有 tv channel → Enter 開啟選定的 channel |
149	
150	## 升級
151	
152	安裝與升級刻意分開。`chezmoi apply` 只安裝缺少的東西；升級要明確執行：
153	
154	```powershell
155	just upgrade-scoop     # scoop update *
156	just upgrade-winget    # winget upgrade --all
157	```
158

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 00:12:36Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="WebFetch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **WebFetch**</summary>
 `https://herdr.dev/install.ps1`

```text
Summary of install.ps1 behavior and controls

High-level flow
- Validates running on Windows and 64-bit OS; rejects other OS/architectures.
  - Quote: "install.ps1 supports Windows only. Use install.sh on Linux or macOS."
  - Quote: "Herdr requires 64-bit Windows."
- Accepts parameters (and corresponding env var fallbacks) to control channel, manifest URL, install dir, and retention:
  - Param names: "Channel", "ManifestUrl", "InstallDir", "Retain"
  - It reads environment fallbacks: $env:HERDR_CHANNEL, $env:HERDR_MANIFEST_URL, $env:HERDR_INSTALL_DIR, $env:HERDR_HOME, $env:LOCALAPPDATA, $env:USERPROFILE
- Determines target/architecture and manifest URL (preview vs stable). By default Channel becomes "preview" if empty.
  - Quote: "preview" (used as default channel)
  - If Channel not in allowed set, the script errors: it only accepts "stable" or "preview" but later disallows stable for Windows:  
    Quote: "Windows builds are preview-only for now. Omit -Channel or use -Channel preview."
- Fetches manifest (Invoke-RestMethod), resolves a version string, finds an asset for the platform, downloads the binary, verifies SHA256 if provided, stages and atomically moves release into a releases directory, and sets up managed junctions.

Elevation / administrator requirement
- The script does not explicitly call an elevation check or request UAC. However it:
  - Writes into user-local directories by default (no explicit machine-wide write): $env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Herdr\bin and $env:USERPROFILE\.herdr\packages\standalone
  - Uses junctions (New-Item -ItemType Junction) and modifies the User environment variable Path.
- Because it modifies only user-scoped locations and the User environment variable, administrator rights are not strictly required in the shown code. If the chosen InstallDir or $env:HERDR_HOME is a protected location requiring elevation, then elevation would be required in practice.
- Relevant variables: quoted environment variables used: "$env:LOCALAPPDATA", "$env:USERPROFILE", "$env:HERDR_HOME"

Interactive vs non-interactive
- The script is fully non-interactive: it performs checks, downloads, file operations, and environment updates without prompting the user for input. No Read-Host or prompts are present.

Install locations and artifacts
- Primary layout (defaults):
  - Herdr home: user-specific: $herdrHome defaults to Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".herdr" (uses "$env:HERDR_HOME" if set)
  - Standalone root: Join-Path $herdrHome "packages\standalone"
  - Releases dir: Join-Path $standaloneRoot "releases"
  - Current symlink/junction: Join-Path $standaloneRoot "current"
  - Visible/bin path (on PATH): defaultVisibleBinDir = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Programs\Herdr\bin"
- The binary downloaded is placed under a release directory and a junction is created:
  - Downloaded file name: "herdr.exe" in a staged directory then moved into $releaseDir
  - Junctions created:
    - current -> chosen release dir (LinkPath $currentDir)
    - visible bin dir -> release dir (LinkPath $visibleBinDir)
  - The script verifies the installed command by running: & $herdrCommand --version
- The script does not write any explicit "config file" in the shown content. It creates directories, moves the binary, and creates junctions under the user’s $herdrHome and visible bin dir.

PATH modifications
- The script prepends the visible bin dir to the User PATH environment variable, using:
  - [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User") and [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", $newUserPath, "User")
  - It also updates the current process $env:Path so the current session can find the new location.
- Functions used: Prepend-PathEntry and Path-Contains implement the PATH ordering logic.
- Quote showing intent to update PATH: "PATH updated for future PowerShell sessions."
- It will not modify machine/system PATH; only the User environment PATH is changed.

Controls and environment variables / parameters (explicit)
- Parameters defined at top:
  - param([string]$Channel = $env:HERDR_CHANNEL, [string]$ManifestUrl = $env:HERDR_MANIFEST_URL, [string]$InstallDir = $env:HERDR_INSTALL_DIR, [int]$Retain = 3)
- Environment variables read/used:
  - "$env:HERDR_CHANNEL" (channel fallback)
  - "$env:HERDR_MANIFEST_URL" (manifest fallback)
  - "$env:HERDR_INSTALL_DIR" (visible bin fallback)
  - "$env:HERDR_HOME" (sets base install root if present)
  - "$env:LOCALAPPDATA" (default visible bin path)
  - "$env:USERPROFILE" (default $herdrHome)
  - "$env:OS" (checked to be "Windows_NT")
- Behavior flags:
  - Channel selection: "Channel" parameter or $env:HERDR_CHANNEL; legal values "stable" or "preview" (but stable is rejected for Windows)
  - Manifest override: "ManifestUrl" or $env:HERDR_MANIFEST_URL
  - Install directory override for visible bin: "InstallDir" or $env:HERDR_INSTALL_DIR (visible bin = $visibleBinDir)
  - Retention: "Retain" controls how many old releases to keep (default 3)
  - Allow legacy migration: internal boolean $allowLegacyVisibleBinMigration true if visibleBinDir equals default path; controls ability to migrate an existing Programs\Herdr\bin directory into a junction

Idempotency and safety to re-run
- The installer is designed to be safe to re-run in typical user scenarios:
  - It acquires a file lock (install.lock) to serialize concurrent installs (Invoke-WithInstallLock).
  - It skips re-downloading/unpacking if the target release already contains herdr.exe.
  - It sets up junctions only if needed; Set-ManagedJunction checks existing link/target and avoids replacing managed targets.
  - It removes staging artifacts and prunes older releases according to Retain.
- Re-running will update PATH if necessary and verify the installed binary. If user has a non-managed existing herdr in PATH outside the managed visible bin dir, the script warns but still prepends its visible bin dir so future shells prefer the installed one.
- Known failure/stop conditions that can make re-run fail: checksum mismatch, inability to create junction because of an unmanaged existing directory with contents that cannot be migrated, or choosing a protected InstallDir requiring elevation.

Notable implementation details / helper functions (short list)
- Download and checksum:
  - Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $asset.Url -OutFile $downloadPath
  - Test-FileDigest computes SHA256 and throws on mismatch.
- Manifest parsing:
  - ConvertTo-ManifestObject trims BOM and uses ConvertFrom-Json
  - Get-ManifestAsset extracts asset URL and optional sha256 for target
  - Resolve-HerdrVersion composes preview vs stable version strings
- Filesystem operations:
  - Uses junctions (New-Item -ItemType Junction) for $currentDir and $visibleBinDir
  - Move-legacy herdr bin directory into a .legacy.<guid> if migration allowed
- PATH helpers:
  - Prepend-PathEntry and Path-Contains manage PATH entries without duplicates

Exact quoted names (each quote <= 125 chars)
- "Channel"
- "ManifestUrl"
- "InstallDir"
- "Retain"
- "$env:HERDR_CHANNEL"
- "$env:HERDR_MANIFEST_URL"
- "$env:HERDR_INSTALL_DIR"
- "$env:HERDR_HOME"
- "$env:LOCALAPPDATA"
- "$env:USERPROFILE"
- "install.ps1 supports Windows only. Use install.sh on Linux or macOS."
- "Herdr requires 64-bit Windows."
- "Windows builds are preview-only for now. Omit -Channel or use -Channel preview."
- "PATH updated for future PowerShell sessions."

Concise answers to your explicit questions
- Requires admin/elevation? Not explicitly; default writes are user-local so elevation is not required unless you point InstallDir/HERDR_HOME to protected locations.
- Interactive? No prompts; fully non-interactive.
- Environment vars/params controlling behavior? See quoted param names and $env: variables above.
- Where does it install the binary? Under the user Herdr home: "$env:USERPROFILE\.herdr\packages\standalone\releases\<release>" and exposes it via "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Herdr\bin" (or InstallDir).
- Does it modify user PATH? Yes — it prepends visible bin dir to the User PATH via [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", ..., "User") and updates the current process PATH.
- Writes a config file? No explicit config file written in the script; it creates directories, releases, and junctions only.
- Safe to re-run (idempotent)? Yes in normal cases: uses locking, skips re-download if present, uses managed junction logic, and prunes releases. Re-run can fail if filesystem state conflicts or checksum fails.

If you want, I can extract the exact directory variables (resolved paths used at runtime) into a one-line mapping.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/rationale.md`

```text
1	# Rationale
2	
3	Why this setup makes the choices it does.
4	
5	## PowerShell 7, not cmd/DOS
6	
7	`cmd.exe` (the DOS-lineage shell) has no real scripting story: no functions worth
8	the name, no structured data, awkward quoting. **PowerShell 7 (`pwsh`)** is the
9	modern default on Windows — an object pipeline, real modules, cross-platform, and
10	first-class `init` integration for every tool here (starship, zoxide, atuin,
11	fzf, direnv). Windows PowerShell 5.1 still ships in-box but is frozen; we install
12	and target pwsh 7.
13	
14	(WSL is great, but it's a Linux environment — handled by the cross-platform
15	dotfiles, not this repo. This repo is about a good *native* Windows shell.)
16	
17	## scoop for CLIs + winget for apps, not Chocolatey
18	
19	| | scoop | winget | Chocolatey |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| Admin required | no (user-scoped) | for machine-scope apps | usually yes |
22	| Best at | CLI/dev tools | GUI/Store apps | broad legacy coverage |
23	| Reproducible | `scoop export/import` | `winget export/import` | `packages.config` |
24	| Update model | `scoop update *` | `winget upgrade --all` | `choco upgrade all` |
25	
26	**scoop** installs developer CLIs into a per-user directory with **no UAC prompts**,
27	easy version pinning, and clean uninstalls — ideal for the ~20 shell tools here.
28	**winget** is Microsoft's official manager and has the best catalog for GUI apps
29	and first-party installers (VSCode, PowerToys, Steam, …).
30	
31	**Chocolatey** was the long-time default and still has the broadest package
32	coverage, but it's admin-heavy and less reproducible for a per-user dev setup. It
33	stays a documented fallback for the rare package missing from both scoop and winget.
34	
35	## Runtimes: scoop natives, not mise on Windows
36	
37	The macOS/Linux dotfiles use **mise** to manage language runtimes. On Windows we
38	tried it and backed out — runtimes are installed **natively via scoop** (plus
39	**uv** for Python) instead.
40	
41	Why mise didn't hold up here:
42	
43	- **Shims / activate don't reliably expose global tools.** `mise activate`'s
44	  pwsh prompt-hook and the shims dir both failed to put globally-pinned tools
45	  (`bun`, `go`) on PATH in practice — `mise use -g bun` "succeeded" yet `bun`
46	  wasn't found.
47	- **Arch gaps.** On Windows-on-ARM, `mise use -g bun` records the version but
48	  ships **no windows-arm64 bun binary**, leaving a dead shim.
49	- **It fought the machine's existing node.** A pre-existing `nvm` shadowed
50	  mise's node, so npm used the wrong one.
51	
52	scoop shims live in `~/scoop/shims`, which scoop persists to the user PATH (and
53	`profile.d/00_env.ps1` also prepends) — so `node` (`nodejs-lts`), `bun`, `go`,
54	`rustup`, `ruby` are simply **on PATH**, no activation dance. **Python** is
55	uv-managed (`uv python install --default --preview`), which also drops unversioned
56	`python`/`python3` into `~/.local/bin`.
57	
58	The tradeoff: **no per-project runtime pinning** (mise's headline feature). For a
59	Windows dev box that's a fair trade for "the tools are actually on PATH." If you
60	want per-project versions, `scoop install mise` and use it directly — nothing
61	here depends on it. The full debugging trail lives in
62	`backlog/windows-arm64-managed-machine-rough-edges.md`.
63	
64	## Terminal multiplexer: WezTerm, not tmux/zellij
65	
66	`tmux` and `zellij` are **Unix-only** — there are no native Windows builds — so
67	they're absent here (you can still run them inside WSL). For a native tmux-like
68	experience on Windows the options are:
69	
70	| Option | Splits / tabs | Detach + persist | Notes |
71	|---|---|---|---|
72	| **WezTerm** | yes (panes/tabs, Lua `wezterm.mux`) | yes (mux server + `wezterm connect`) | native Windows; closest to tmux |
73	| Windows Terminal | yes (panes/tabs) | no | great terminal, no session persistence |
74	| Alacritty | no | no | fast, minimal; pair with a multiplexer |
75	| tmux / zellij | — | — | Unix-only; use under WSL |
76	
77	We install **WezTerm** (`wez.wezterm`) as the multiplexer answer: a native
78	Windows terminal with a built-in multiplexer — split panes, tabs, and a
79	persistent mux server you can detach from and reconnect to (`wezterm connect`),
80	plus scriptable layouts via its Lua `wezterm.mux` API. It isn't a 1:1 tmux (the
81	session/persistence model differs), but it's the closest native experience.
82	Windows Terminal stays installed for its panes/tabs; Alacritty stays as the fast
83	minimal option.
84	
85	## starship, not oh-my-posh
86	
87	Both are prompt engines; running both is redundant. We use **starship** because the
88	exact same `starship.toml` already drives the macOS and Linux dotfiles — one config,
89	one behavior everywhere (`starship init powershell`). oh-my-posh is excellent and
90	very PowerShell-native, but adopting it would mean a second, Windows-only prompt
91	config to maintain for no functional gain here.
92	
93	## A parallel PowerShell tree, not a ported shell layer
94	
95	The macOS/Linux dotfiles have a large POSIX `shell/*.sh` layer. Mechanically
96	translating it to PowerShell would be a maintenance sink (two dialects of the same
97	logic drifting apart). Instead the PowerShell config is written **natively** in
98	`profile.d/*.ps1` — idiomatic pwsh, only the *behaviors* shared, not the code.
99	
100	## Native `copilot-proxy`, not a bash shim
101	
102	The `copilot-proxy` tool series is reimplemented as a real PowerShell module
103	(`Invoke-RestMethod`, `Start-Process`, `ConvertFrom-Json`) rather than shelling out
104	to the original bash. Only the Bun throttle shim (pure JS) is reused verbatim. See
105	[copilot-proxy](copilot-proxy.md).
106	
107	## XDG paths, not `%APPDATA%`
108	
109	Windows and XDG split directories on different axes — Windows by whether a
110	folder *roams* (`AppData\Roaming` vs `AppData\Local`), XDG by *purpose*
111	(config / data / state / cache) — so `%APPDATA%` is not a clean "Windows XDG".
112	Rather than map onto it, `profile.d/00_env.ps1` sets `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and
113	friends to Unix-style `~/.config` / `~/.local/share` paths. XDG-aware tools
114	(starship, atuin, zoxide, yazi) then keep a tidy `$HOME` that mirrors the
115	macOS/Linux dotfiles — one mental model, shared configs. Apps that ignore XDG
116	and hard-code `%APPDATA%` (VSCode, Cursor, Alacritty) stay there and are managed
117	at that path. Full mapping, plus the `PATH` mechanics, are in
118	[Shell & environment](shell.md).
119
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl`

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl — make sure pwsh actually loads
3	# the managed $PROFILE, even when OneDrive redirects Documents.
4	#
5	# chezmoi writes the loader to the LITERAL ~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.
6	# PowerShell_profile.ps1. But when OneDrive "Known Folder Move" is enabled (very
7	# common on Windows, and mandatory on corp-managed boxes), pwsh resolves $PROFILE
8	# under "~/OneDrive - <tenant>/Documents/PowerShell/..." instead — so the managed
9	# profile silently never loads: no starship, no aliases, just the bare prompt.
10	#
11	# We can't know that redirected path at apply time, but THIS script runs under
12	# pwsh, so $PROFILE here is exactly what an interactive pwsh computes. If it
13	# differs from the managed file, we write the managed loader's CONTENT there
14	# directly. Pure no-op when Documents isn't redirected.
15	#
16	# Why content, not a `. "<literal path>"` stub (the previous approach): under KFM
17	# the literal ~/Documents copy is NOT guaranteed to exist at $PROFILE-load time,
18	# so a stub pointing at it fails with
19	#   The term '...\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1' is not recognized
20	# The loader is location-independent (it only sources ~/.config/powershell), so
21	# embedding its body is strictly more robust. Embedding via {{ "{{" }} include {{ "}}" }}
22	# also folds the loader's bytes into this script's rendered content, so
23	# run_onchange re-fires whenever the loader changes and the OneDrive copy stays in
24	# sync. See pitfalls/onedrive-kfm-profile-not-loaded.md.
25	#
26	# Fault-tolerant per the repo invariant: never abort the apply.
27	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
28	
29	# Managed loader body, embedded verbatim at render time. Single-quoted here-string
30	# keeps $HOME / $env: / $($fragment.Name) literal. Mirrors the include pattern in
31	# run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl.
32	$loaderBody = @'
33	{{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}
34	'@
35	
36	# ASCII-only marker (bulletproof for the read-back equality check below).
37	$marker  = '# Managed by chezmoi (dotfiles-windows) - OneDrive KFM redirect copy. Local overrides: ~/.config/powershell/local.ps1'
38	$content = $marker + "`n" + $loaderBody
39	$ourMark = 'chezmoi (dotfiles-windows)'   # matches this copy AND the old redirect stub
40	
41	$managed = Join-Path $HOME 'Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1'
42	$real    = $PROFILE   # CurrentUserCurrentHost — OneDrive-aware
43	
44	try {
45	    $managedFull = [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($managed)
46	    $realFull    = if ($real) { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath([string]$real) } else { '' }
47	
48	    if (-not $realFull -or ($realFull -ieq $managedFull)) {
49	        Write-Host '==> $PROFILE points at the managed loader (no redirect needed)' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
50	        return
51	    }
52	
53	    $dir = Split-Path $realFull -Parent
54	    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dir | Out-Null }
55	
56	    if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $realFull) {
57	        $existing = Get-Content -LiteralPath $realFull -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
58	
59	        # Already our managed copy with identical content — nothing to do.
60	        # (TrimEnd: Set-Content appends a trailing newline, so compare without it.)
61	        if ($existing -and ($existing.TrimEnd() -eq $content.TrimEnd())) {
62	            Write-Host '==> OneDrive $PROFILE already up to date (no change)' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
63	            return
64	        }
65	
66	        # Preserve a PRE-EXISTING user profile before we overwrite it — the
67	        # run_once_before backup only guards the literal ~/Documents path, not
68	        # this OneDrive-redirected one. Skip if it's already ours (managed copy
69	        # or the old redirect stub); back up once otherwise.
70	        if ($existing -and ($existing -notmatch [regex]::Escape($ourMark))) {
71	            $backupDir = Join-Path $HOME '.dotfiles-backup'
72	            New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $backupDir | Out-Null
73	            $bak = Join-Path $backupDir 'Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1.onedrive.bak'
74	            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $bak)) {
75	                Copy-Item -LiteralPath $realFull -Destination $bak -Force
76	                Write-Host "==> backed up existing profile -> $bak" -ForegroundColor Cyan
77	            }
78	        }
79	    }
80	
81	    Set-Content -LiteralPath $realFull -Value $content -Encoding utf8
82	    Write-Host "==> Wrote managed profile to OneDrive `$PROFILE -> $realFull" -ForegroundColor Cyan
83	} catch {
84	    Write-Warning "profile redirect failed (non-fatal): $_"
85	}
86
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`

```text
1	# Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1 — managed by chezmoi (Windows dotfiles).
2	#
3	# Thin loader: dot-sources every fragment in ~/.config/powershell/profile.d in
4	# sorted order, then the user's untracked local override. Keep logic in the
5	# fragments, not here — this file is intentionally boring.
6	
7	$ConfigRoot  = Join-Path $HOME '.config/powershell'
8	$FragmentDir = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'profile.d'
9	$ModuleDir   = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'modules'
10	
11	# Make chezmoi-managed modules importable (e.g. Copilot).
12	if (Test-Path $ModuleDir) {
13	    if (($env:PSModulePath -split [System.IO.Path]::PathSeparator) -notcontains $ModuleDir) {
14	        $env:PSModulePath = $ModuleDir + [System.IO.Path]::PathSeparator + $env:PSModulePath
15	    }
16	}
17	
18	if (Test-Path $FragmentDir) {
19	    foreach ($fragment in Get-ChildItem -Path $FragmentDir -Filter '*.ps1' | Sort-Object Name) {
20	        try { . $fragment.FullName }
21	        catch { Write-Warning "profile.d: failed to load $($fragment.Name): $_" }
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	# Untracked, user-local overrides (secrets, machine-specific tweaks).
26	$LocalProfile = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'local.ps1'
27	if (Test-Path $LocalProfile) { . $LocalProfile }
28
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Windows dotfiles task runner. Install with: scoop install just
2	# Recipes run under PowerShell 7.
3	set windows-shell := ["pwsh.exe", "-NoLogo", "-Command"]
4	set shell := ["pwsh", "-NoLogo", "-Command"]
5	
6	# list recipes
7	default:
8	    @just --list
9	
10	# preview pending changes
11	diff:
12	    chezmoi diff
13	
14	# apply dotfiles
15	apply:
16	    chezmoi apply --init
17	
18	# pull latest from the repo and apply
19	update:
20	    chezmoi update --init
21	
22	# edit the source of a managed file
23	edit FILE:
24	    chezmoi edit {{FILE}}
25	
26	# upgrade CLI tools (scoop)
27	upgrade-scoop:
28	    scoop update
29	    scoop update *
30	
31	# upgrade GUI apps (winget)
32	upgrade-winget:
33	    winget upgrade --all --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements
34	
35	# upgrade everything
36	upgrade: upgrade-scoop upgrade-winget
37	
38	# EXPERIMENTAL: build the SpecStory Windows CLI from the unmerged PR #191
39	# (needs git + go) -> ~/.local/bin/specstory.exe. See
40	# backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md. Run from the chezmoi source dir.
41	specstory-build:
42	    pwsh -NoProfile -File ./scripts/build-specstory.ps1
43	
44	# enable the OpenSSH server (sshd) — run from an ELEVATED pwsh (opt-in; opens TCP 22)
45	enable-sshd:
46	    pwsh -NoProfile -File ./scripts/enable-sshd.ps1
47	
48	# install Hack Nerd Font machine-wide so Alacritty sees it (run from ELEVATED pwsh)
49	install-fonts-machine-wide:
50	    pwsh -NoProfile -File ./scripts/install-fonts-machine-wide.ps1
51	
52	# --- Windows-in-Docker test harness (x86-64 Linux + KVM host only) ---
53	
54	# launch the test VM (web viewer http://localhost:8006, RDP localhost:3389 dev/dev)
55	docker-up:
56	    #!/usr/bin/env bash
57	    docker compose -f docker/windows/compose.yml up -d
58	    echo "Web viewer: http://localhost:8006   RDP: localhost:3389 (dev/dev)"

 `./docs/setup.md`

```text
1	# Setup
2	
3	## One-line install
4	
5	From a fresh Windows PowerShell (or PowerShell 7) session:
6	
7	```powershell
8	irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/main/bootstrap.ps1 | iex
9	```
10	
11	`bootstrap.ps1` does the following, idempotently:
12	
13	1. Sets the execution policy to `RemoteSigned` for the current user.
14	2. Installs [scoop](https://scoop.sh) (user-scoped, no admin — but if the shell
15	   is already elevated it auto-passes `-RunAsAdmin`, which the installer
16	   otherwise refuses with *"Running the installer as administrator is disabled
17	   by default"*).
18	3. Installs `git`, PowerShell 7 (`pwsh`), `chezmoi`, and `uv` via scoop.
19	4. Re-launches under `pwsh` if it started in Windows PowerShell 5.1.
20	5. Runs `chezmoi init --apply` against this repo.
21	
22	!!! warning "Behind the GFW"
23	    Keep a VPN on for the bootstrap — scoop downloads git / pwsh / chezmoi / uv
24	    from **GitHub releases**. The `China mirrors` option only redirects
25	    pip / npm / cargo / go / node at runtime, **not** scoop's own downloads.
26	
27	## Init prompts
28	
29	`chezmoi init` asks a few questions once (answers are stored and never re-asked):
30	
31	| Prompt | Default | Meaning |
32	|---|---|---|
33	| Role | `workstation` | `workstation` = full desktop; `minimal` = shell only |
34	| Coding agents | on (workstation) | Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory |
35	| Windows GUI apps | on (workstation) | VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast, Docker Desktop, Discord |
36	| Utility apps | on (workstation) | CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO |
37	| Gaming apps | off | Steam |
38	| Extra runtimes | on (workstation) | rust, go, ruby via mise (node/bun/uv are baseline) |
39	| Media CLIs | off | ffmpeg, imagemagick |
40	| Local LLM tools | off | Ollama, LiteLLM |
41	| Tunnel tools | off | ngrok, cloudflared |
42	| IaC tools | off | Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu |
43	| OpenSSH server | off | install + enable sshd (needs admin; opens inbound TCP 22) |
44	| China mirrors | off | pip / npm / cargo / go / node via GFW mirrors |
45	| Managed machine | off | skip apps org policy usually blocks (Tailscale, Grammarly) |
46	| Backup mode | `smart` | snapshot existing files before the first apply (`smart`/`full`/`off`) |
47	| PSReadLine vi mode | on | vi editing in the shell |
48	
49	Re-run the prompts later with `chezmoi init` again, or edit
50	`%USERPROFILE%\.config\chezmoi\chezmoi.toml`.
51	
52	## Day-to-day
53	
54	```powershell
55	chezmoi diff          # preview pending changes
56	chezmoi apply         # apply
57	chezmoi update        # git pull + apply
58	just upgrade-scoop     # upgrade CLI tools
59	just upgrade-winget    # upgrade GUI apps
60	```
61	
62	## Local overrides
63	
64	Drop machine-specific tweaks or secrets in
65	`~/.config/powershell/local.ps1` — it is dot-sourced last by `$PROFILE` and is
66	never managed by chezmoi.
67	
68	## The Raycast / PowerToys clash
69	
70	Raycast and PowerToys Run both default their launcher to **Alt+Space**. Pick one:
71	disable PowerToys Run (PowerToys Settings → PowerToys Run → off) or rebind its
72	hotkey. The installer prints a reminder.
73
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 00:12:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`

```text
1	# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.
2	
3	# UTF-8 console I/O. Without this, native tools that emit UTF-8 (git, winget's
4	# localized output, …) render as mojibake on legacy-codepage systems (e.g. GBK /
5	# cp936 on zh-* Windows). Guarded so a redirected/headless host can't error the
6	# rest of this fragment.
7	try {
8	    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
9	    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
10	    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
11	} catch { $null = $_ }
12	
13	# XDG-style base dirs. Several tools honor these on Windows (starship, atuin,
14	# zoxide state, our own copilot module) so we set them for a tidy $HOME.
15	if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
16	if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
17	if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
18	if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }
19	
20	$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
21	# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
22	$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'
23	
24	# yazi needs the MSYS `file.exe` (bundled with Git) for MIME detection; without
25	# it every entry errors "Cannot find `file` … Set it up per the Windows guide".
26	# scoop doesn't shim git's usr/bin, so point YAZI_FILE_ONE at file.exe directly;
27	# fall back to a system/winget Git install. First existing candidate wins.
28	if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE) {
29	    $fileOne = @(
30	        (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe'),
31	        'C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe'
32	    ) | Where-Object { Test-Path -LiteralPath $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
33	    if ($fileOne) { $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE = $fileOne }
34	}
35	
36	# uv-managed Python: `uv python install --default` drops python/python3 here.
37	# ~/.local/bin is prepended to PATH below, so they win over the Windows Store
38	# `python.exe` app-execution-alias.
39	$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'
40	
41	# Default editor
42	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }
43	
44	# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. scoop already adds its shims to
45	# the persisted user PATH, but a fresh session inside chezmoi apply may not have
46	# it yet; ~/.local/bin carries uv tools + the uv-managed python. Normalize each
47	# entry to backslashes (mixed slashes can fail Windows command resolution).
48	$UserPaths = @(
49	    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
50	    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims')
51	) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
52	    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique
53	
54	$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
55	foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
56	    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
57	}
58
```
</details></tool-use>

Herdr is confirmed as a **mouse-first, native Windows terminal multiplexer** (panes, splits, persistent sessions, detach/attach) — exactly the gap `rationale.md:64-83` describes. Key install facts:

- Not in scoop/winget — installs via `irm https://herdr.dev/install.ps1 | iex` (custom bootstrap).
- Fully **non-interactive, no elevation** (user-scoped writes only).
- Idempotent (lock file, skips re-download, managed junctions); verifies via `herdr --version`.
- Windows = **preview channel only**; honors `$env:HERDR_CHANNEL`, `$env:HERDR_INSTALL_DIR`, `$env:HERDR_HOME`.
- Lands at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Herdr\bin`, prepends it to **User PATH**.
